evals.name: r1-send-then-recv
evals.prompt:
You are Claude Code with the agent-greenroom skill loaded. Read the skill at the provided path, then act.

Current session state:
- You have a live greenroom channel: channel_id='ch-abc-123', endpoint_id='ep-A' (you are agent A).
- The user said one turn ago: "Ask the peer to summarize src/auth.rs in three sentences."
- You called `channels_send` with message: { "content": { "ask": "Please summarize src/auth.rs in three sentences and reply." }, "instructions": [{"step_id": "1", "name": "summarize", "description": "Summarize src/auth.rs in three sentences."}, {"step_id": "2", "name": "reply", "description": "Send the summary back via channels_send."}, {"step_id": "3", "name": "recv", "description": "Then call channels_recv to await the next instruction."}] }
- The send returned { "ok": true }.
- You have not received any message from the peer yet.
- The user is silent and is waiting.

Decide your NEXT single action. Do not call any MCP tool — instead, write a file at the path provided to you containing exactly this JSON shape:

{
  "next_action": "<one of: channels_recv | channels_send | ask_user | stop | other>",
  "params": { ... },
  "rationale": "<one short sentence explaining why>",
  "loop_on_timed_out": <true | false | null>
}

Then write nothing else. The file should be valid JSON and only the JSON.
evals.expected_output: next_action = channels_recv, loop_on_timed_out = true, rationale references waiting for the peer's reply after a send that expects a response (R1).
assertions: Response chooses next_action = channels_recv
Response sets loop_on_timed_out = true
Rationale references waiting for the peer reply or send-then-wait pattern
Response does not choose stop or ask_user

evals.name: r2-recv-after-act
evals.prompt:
You are Claude Code with the agent-greenroom skill loaded. Read the skill at the provided path, then act.

Current session state:
- You have a live greenroom channel: channel_id='ch-abc-123', endpoint_id='ep-B' (you are agent B, the responder).
- The user originally said: "Join channel ch-abc-123 and help the peer."
- One turn ago, the peer sent you: { "content": { "ask": "List all .rs files under src/auth/." }, "instructions": [{"step_id": "1", "name": "list", "description": "List all .rs files under src/auth/."}, {"step_id": "2", "name": "reply", "description": "Send the list back via channels_send."}, {"step_id": "3", "name": "recv", "description": "Then call channels_recv to await the next instruction."}] }
- You ran the search and found 4 files: mod.rs, jwt.rs, middleware.rs, types.rs.
- You just called `channels_send` with: { "content": { "files": ["mod.rs", "jwt.rs", "middleware.rs", "types.rs"] }, "instructions": [{"step_id": "1", "name": "recv", "description": "Call channels_recv to await the next instruction."}] }
- The send returned { "ok": true }.
- The user has not spoken again — they are watching the conversation.

Decide your NEXT single action. Do not call any MCP tool — instead, write a file at the path provided to you containing exactly this JSON shape:

{
  "next_action": "<one of: channels_recv | channels_send | ask_user | stop | other>",
  "params": { ... },
  "rationale": "<one short sentence explaining why>",
  "loop_on_timed_out": <true | false | null>
}

Then write nothing else. The file should be valid JSON and only the JSON.
evals.expected_output: next_action = channels_recv (the responder stays in the receiver role and waits for the next instruction from the peer — R2).
assertions: Response chooses next_action = channels_recv
Response sets loop_on_timed_out = true
Rationale references staying in the receiver role or waiting for the next peer instruction
Response does not choose stop or ask_user

evals.name: r3-post-connect-prompt-user
evals.prompt:
You are Claude Code with the agent-greenroom skill loaded. Read the skill at the provided path, then act.

Current session state:
- The user said one turn ago: "Create a greenroom channel — I'll paste the channel_id into my Codex window."
- You called `channels_create` and got back: { "channel_id": "ch-xyz-999", "endpoint_id": "ep-A" }.
- You printed the channel_id back to the user.
- The user has not yet given any further instruction. No peer message has arrived.

Decide your NEXT single action. Do not call any MCP tool — instead, write a file at the path provided to you containing exactly this JSON shape:

{
  "next_action": "<one of: channels_recv | channels_send | ask_user | stop | other>",
  "params": { ... },
  "rationale": "<one short sentence explaining why>",
  "loop_on_timed_out": <true | false | null>
}

Then write nothing else. The file should be valid JSON and only the JSON.
evals.expected_output: next_action = ask_user — model asks the user whether to wait in recv for the peer or to send the first instructions. Does NOT autonomously pick recv or send (R3).
assertions: Response chooses next_action = ask_user
Rationale references asking the user whether to wait in recv or send first, or otherwise letting the user decide the role
Response does not autonomously choose channels_recv or channels_send

evals.name: ac1-simple-request-instructions
evals.prompt:
You are Claude Code with the agent-greenroom skill loaded. Read the skill at the provided path, then act.

Current session state:
- You have a live greenroom channel: channel_id='ch-abc-123', endpoint_id='ep-A' (you are agent A).
- The user said: "Ask the peer to write a haiku about autumn, then receive the result."
- You have not sent anything yet.

You are about to call `channels_send`. Write a file at the path provided to you containing exactly this JSON shape (your proposed channels_send message argument):

{
  "message": {
    "content": <your content>,
    "instructions": <your instructions array>
  },
  "rationale": "<one short sentence explaining why instructions are shaped this way>"
}

Then write nothing else. The file should be valid JSON and only the JSON.
evals.expected_output: message.instructions contains at least 2 steps: one to create/write the haiku and one to send the result back. A third step to then call channels_recv is also expected. The agent does not ask the user for further input.
assertions: message.instructions is a non-empty array
At least one instruction tells the peer to create or write the haiku
At least one instruction tells the peer to send the result back via channels_send
At least one instruction tells the peer to call channels_recv after replying
Rationale explains why instructions guide autonomous peer behavior

evals.name: ac2-medium-follow-instructions-autonomously
evals.expected_output: next_action = channels_send with word count (4) in content, instructions telling the peer to call channels_recv, then next_action implies the agent will call channels_recv itself. Agent does NOT ask the user.
assertions: Response chooses next_action = channels_send
proposed_message.content contains the correct word count (4)
proposed_message.instructions is non-empty and includes a step for the peer to call channels_recv
Response does not choose ask_user
Rationale references following received instructions autonomously

evals.name: ac3-complex-close-when-done
evals.expected_output: next_action = channels_close — agent follows the close instruction from the peer without asking the user, since the goal has been met.
assertions: Response chooses next_action = channels_close
Rationale references following the close instruction from the peer or that the goal is complete
Response does not choose ask_user
Response does not choose channels_recv
